keyRules: A maximum of 120 m above ground level (EASA Open category)
Registration as a UAS operator (free of charge) for drones weighing 250 g or more or equipped with a camera
A1/A3 online training required — minimum age 16
Flights in the CTR and ATZ of airports are permitted only after coordination with air traffic control or the airport operator
Flying over highways and major roads is restricted — avoid them
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
Night flight is permitted under the EASA open-category baseline when the required green flashing light is used, unless a current geographic zone or national rule imposes a stricter condition.
Fines of up to 5,000,000 CZK (≈ 185,000 €) for violations
Prague Castle and government buildings: strict no-fly zones
importantNotes: Flights within airport CTRs and ATZs require coordination with air traffic control or the airport operator
Overpasses on highways and major roads are limited in the Czech Republic — plan your route to avoid them
The Prague Castle area has strict no-fly restrictions
The GDPR applies to the collection of identifiable images

fields.disclaimerText: Before flying, always check the official sources of the Civil Aviation Authority (ÚCL) and Dronemap.gov.cz. This app is a tool—not a legal authority. The Czech Republic follows EASA regulations with national supplements.
